content.text:
Just as normal subgroups allow quotient groups, ideals allow us to 
construct quotient rings. The elements of the quotient ring are cosets, 
and the operations are well-defined because of the absorption property.

content.title: Quotient Ring
content.content:
Let $I$ be an ideal of a ring $R$. The set of cosets $R/I = \{a + I : 
a \in R\}$ forms a ring under:
$$
 (a + I) + (b + I) &= (a + b) + I \\
 (a + I)(b + I) &= ab + I
$$
This is called the **quotient ring** of $R$ 
by $I$.
content.label: Theorem

content.content:
Suppose $a + I = a' + I$ and $b + I = b' + I$. Then $a' = a + i$ and 
$b' = b + j$ for some $i, j \in I$.

$a'b' = (a + i)(b + j) = ab + aj + ib + ij$

Since $I$ is an ideal: $aj, ib, ij \in I$, so $aj + ib + ij \in I$.

Thus $a'b' + I = ab + I$.

content.title: $\Z/n\Z$
content.problem:
The quotient ring $\Z/n\Z$ consists of cosets $\{0 + n\Z, 1 + n\Z, 
\ldots, (n-1) + n\Z\}$ with modular arithmetic. This is precisely 
$\Z_n$.

content.title: Quotient by Prime Ideal
content.content:
Let $R$ be a commutative ring with unity and $I$ a proper ideal.

1. $I$ is prime $\Leftrightarrow$ $R/I$ is an integral domain

2. $I$ is maximal $\Leftrightarrow$ $R/I$ is a field
content.label: Theorem

content.content:
$R/I$ has zero divisors iff $\exists\, a, b \notin I$ with $ab \in I$.

This is exactly when $I$ is not prime.

content.title: Consequences
content.problem:
- $\Z_p = \Z/(p)$ is a field when $p$ is prime (since $(p)$ is 
 maximal).

- $\Z_6 = \Z/(6)$ has zero divisors (since $(6)$ is not prime).

- $\R[x]/(x^2 + 1) \cong \C$ (since $(x^2 + 1)$ is maximal in 
 $\R[x]$).

content.title: Constructing $\C$ from $\R$
content.problem:
In $\R[x]$, the ideal $(x^2 + 1)$ is maximal. The quotient:
\[
 \R[x]/(x^2 + 1) \cong \C
\]
by mapping $x + (x^2 + 1) \mapsto i$. Since $x^2 + 1 \mapsto 0$, we 
have $x^2 = -1$ in the quotient, exactly as $i^2 = -1$ in $\C$.

content.title: Finite fields
content.problem:
$\Z_2[x]/(x^2 + x + 1)$ is a field with $4$ elements. The polynomial 
$x^2 + x + 1$ is irreducible over $\Z_2$ (check: it has no roots in 
$\Z_2$).

items: Is $(x^2 - 1)$ a prime ideal in $\R[x]$? A maximal ideal?

marginNotes.text:
He who was seated on the throne said, 
"I am making everything new!"
